steps.title: Use slope‑intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function can be written as $$y = mx + b$$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y‑intercept.
Given $m = 51$, we have $$y = 51x + b$$.
The graph passes through $(0,0)$, so substitute $x=0$, $y=0$:
$$0 = 51 \cdot 0 + b$$
which simplifies to $$b = 0$$.
Thus the equation is $$y = 51x$$.

steps.title: Write the function and match the choice
steps.content:
With $b = 0$, the function is $$f(x) = 51x$$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
